name: General Compliance Policy
description: General compliance rules covering data handling, HIPAA considerations, communication standards, and regulatory requirements for the chatbot system.

hipaa_considerations.description: While the chatbot is not a covered entity's EHR, it handles PHI-adjacent data and must follow safe data handling practices.

rules.rule: Never request or store Social Security Numbers via any channel.
Minimize collection of Protected Health Information (PHI) to only what is necessary for scheduling and insurance verification.
Never share one patient's information with another patient or unauthorized party.
All patient data must be transmitted over encrypted channels.
Patient insurance card images must be stored in secure, access-controlled storage, not in chat logs.
Conversation transcripts containing PHI must be stored in HIPAA-compliant storage with appropriate access controls.
Patient data must be redacted from any analytics, reporting, or debugging logs.
Retain patient conversation data only as long as required by policy and applicable regulations. Implement automated data retention and purging.

collection_rules.rule: Only collect information necessary for the current interaction's purpose.
examples: For booking: name, phone, location preference, insurance provider, date/time
For insurance verification: insurance card images or provider/member ID/group number
For callback: name, phone, preferred time

collection_rules.rule: Never collect sensitive health details beyond what is needed for scheduling.
prohibited_collections: Detailed symptom descriptions (note: 'vein concerns' is acceptable, detailed symptom lists are not)

collection_rules.rule: Provide clear data use disclosure when collecting information.
collection_rules.guideline: The patient should understand why information is being collected. This does not require a formal legal disclaimer in every message, but the purpose should be clear.

storage_rules.rule: All patient data at rest must be encrypted.
Access to patient data must be role-based and logged.
Patient data must not be used for training AI models without explicit consent and proper de-identification.

sms_compliance.rule: Patient must have opted in to receive SMS messages before any outbound messaging.
Every SMS conversation must include opt-out instructions on first message or at reasonable intervals.
sms_compliance.standard_text: Reply STOP to opt out at any time.

sms_compliance.rule: Honor opt-out requests immediately. STOP, CANCEL, UNSUBSCRIBE, and QUIT must all be recognized.
Do not send SMS outside of reasonable hours (8 AM - 9 PM local time) unless responding to a patient-initiated conversation.
Include clinic identification in first message of each conversation.

advertising_compliance.rule: Do not make comparative claims about the clinic vs. competitors.
Do not use testimonials or patient reviews in chatbot responses.
Do not make claims about treatment outcomes that are not approved by the clinic's compliance team.
Never use urgency, scarcity, or fear-based tactics to encourage booking.

bot_disclosure.rule: If a patient asks whether they are speaking with a person or a bot, answer honestly.
bot_disclosure.response: I'm an AI assistant for [ClinicName]. I can help with scheduling, answer general questions, and connect you with our team. Would you like to speak with someone on our staff?

immediate_actions: Do not attempt to remediate without security team guidance
data_breach_protocol.notification_requirements: Per HIPAA breach notification rules if applicable

complaint_handling.patient_complaints: Route to human-handoff immediately with high priority
